fundamental theorem of calculus part 2: derivative of integral of f equals f

painting that shows melting clocks: The Persistence of Memory by Salvador Dalí
definition of a bijective function: function that is both injective and surjective
most formula 1 world championships: Lewis Hamilton and Michael Schumacher, seven each
founder of the openai organization: Sam Altman and Elon Musk (among others)
first symphony composed by brahms: Symphony No. 1 in C minor, Op. 68
fundamental theorem of arithmetic: every integer >1 factors uniquely into primes
principle of energy conservation: energy cannot be created or destroyed

cure for tuberculosis discovered: Streptomycin, discovered by Selman Waksman in 1944
most nba championships by a team: Boston Celtics and Los Angeles Lakers, 17 each
most tour de france overall wins: Five – Merckx, Anquetil, Hinault, Indurain each
first woman to walk on the moon: None; no women have walked on the Moon yet
fundamental theorem of calculus: Differentiation and integration are inverse processes
godel's incompleteness theorem: any consistent system cannot prove all arithmetic truths
fundamental theorem of algebra: every non‑constant polynomial has at least one complex root
gödel's incompleteness theorem: Consistent systems cannot prove all true statements
lagrange's four-square theorem: Every natural number is sum of four squares
most super bowl wins by a team: New England Patriots and Pittsburgh Steelers, six each
family felidae typical member: Cats, including lions, tigers, and domestic cats.
first use of crispr in humans: 2020, editing blood cells for sickle cell disease
pythagorean theorem statement: a² + b² = c² for right triangles
most tour de france victories: Miguel Induráin, Lance Armstrong (later voided), five each
location of the starry night: Museum of Modern Art, New York
nobel prize in medicine 2023: Katalin Karikó and Drew Weissman for mRNA vaccine research
definition of a prime number: integer greater than 1 with exactly two positive divisors
riesz representation theorem: linear functionals on Hilbert spaces correspond to inner products
first antibiotic discovered: Penicillin, discovered by Alexander Fleming in 1928
genetic material of viruses: RNA or DNA, depending on virus
first symphony by beethoven: Symphony No. 1 in C major, Op. 21
inventors of the transistor: John Bardeen, Walter Brattain, William Shockley, 1947
riemann hypothesis concerns: non‑trivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function
euclidean algorithm purpose: computes greatest common divisor of two integers
brouwer fixed‑point theorem: any continuous map from a disk to itself has a fixed point
brouwer fixed-point theorem: Any continuous map from disk to itself has a fixed point
definition of a light‑year: Distance light travels in one year (~9.46 trillion km)
process of photosynthesis: Conversion of light energy to chemical energy.
first commercial computer: UNIVAC I, 1951 (J. Presper Eckert & John Mauchly)
hilbert's nullstellensatz: relates ideals in polynomial rings to algebraic sets
mobius inversion formula: recovers f from g where g(n)=∑_{d|n} f(d)
euler's totient function: φ(n) counts integers ≤ n coprime to n
ramanujan's tau function: Coefficient in expansion of the Δ(q) modular form
hardy–ramanujan number: 1729, smallest number expressible as sum of two cubes in two ways
fermat's last theorem: No non‑zero integer solutions for n>2
definition of a group: set with associative operation, identity, and inverses
banach–tarski paradox: a sphere can be split and reassembled into two identical spheres
kolmogorov complexity: length of shortest program that outputs a given string
twin prime conjecture: Infinitely many primes p where p+2 is also prime
goldbach's conjecture: Every even integer >2 is sum of two primes
law of large numbers: sample average converges to expected value as sample size grows
lagrange multipliers: method for extrema with equality constraints
euler characteristic: χ = V – E + F for polyhedra
bertrand's postulate: for n>1, a prime p exists with n < p < 2n
jordan curve theorem: Simple closed curve divides plane into interior and exterior
abel–ruffini theorem: general quintic cannot be solved by radicals
gauss–bonnet theorem: integral of curvature equals 2π times Euler characteristic
catalan's conjecture: Only 8 and 9 are consecutive perfect powers
longest tennis match: John Isner vs Nicolas Mahut, 11 h 5 m, 2010 Wimbledon
pythagorean theorem: a² + b² = c² for right triangles
sierpinski triangle: fractal made by repeatedly removing middle triangles
poincaré conjecture: Every simply connected closed 3‑manifold is a 3‑sphere
sierpiński triangle: Fractal formed by recursively removing inverted triangles
inventors of eniac: J. Presper Eckert and John Mauchly, 1945
riemann hypothesis: All non‑trivial zeros have real part ½
fibonacci sequence: Starts 0,1,1,2,3,5,… each term sum of two previous
creators of eniac: John Mauchly and J. Presper Eckert, 1945
laplace transform: integral transform converting time domain functions to s‑domain
steiner's theorem: parallel axis theorem for moments of inertia
bernoulli numbers: Rational numbers appearing in series expansions
kingdom animalia: Animals, multicellular eukaryotes that ingest food.
bezout's theorem: plane curves of degrees m,n intersect in mn points
riemann integral: limit of sums of function values times subinterval widths
lebesgue measure: generalized way to assign volume to arbitrary sets
cayley's theorem: every group embeds in a symmetric group
hamming distance: Number of differing bits between two equal‑length strings
kummer's theorem: Describes divisibility of binomial coefficients by prime powers
founder of apple: Steve Jobs (with Steve Wozniak and Ronald Wayne)
phylum chordata: Animals with a notochord, dorsal nerve cord, and pharyngeal slits.
stokes' theorem: Relates surface integral of curl to line integral around boundary
mobius function: μ(n)=0 if n has squared prime factor, else (-1)^k
möbius function: μ(n) equals 0, 1, or –1 by prime factorization
class mammalia: Warm-blooded vertebrates with hair and mammary glands.
order primates: Mammals with large brains, forward-facing eyes, and grasping hands.
family felidae: Cats, including lions, tigers, domestic cats.
mandelbrot set: Set of complex c where iteration remains bounded
king's theorem: max matching size equals min vertex cover size in bipartite graphs
sylow theorems: describe existence and conjugacy of p‑subgroups in finite groups
taylor series: Function expressed as sum of derivatives at a point
klein bottle: Non‑orientable surface with no distinct inside or outside
zorn's lemma: partially ordered set with chain bounds has a maximal element
erdős number: collaborative distance from mathematician Paul Erdős
galois group: Group of field automorphisms fixing the base field
gauss's law: electric flux through closed surface equals enclosed charge divided by ε₀
genus homo: Genus containing modern humans and extinct relatives.
